rulings.Case Title: GARY STRENG VS. KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One And Form Interrogatories No. 17.1 And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defts Keynote Properties, Inc And Robert Camozzi
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF GARY STRENG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set One And Form Interrogatories No. 17.1 And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants Keynote Properties, Inc. and Robert Camozzi Hearing Requried. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. Parties are to report of their progress to the Court at 9:30 a.m. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LISA JOHNSON et al VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action. No opposition filed. Demurrer is overruled as to the 6th, 7th, 9th and 10th causes of action to the extent they are alleged by Plaintiffs solely as tenants against Defendant solely as landlord. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSE MARIE HARRIS VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2013, line 3. Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show likelihood of prevailing on the merits. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EBRAHIM POURA VS. KHADAR EL SHAWA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion To Quash Unlawful Detainer Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT KHADAR EL SHAWA's Re-Notice Of Motion To Quash The Court is treating this motion as demurrer. Defendant generally appeared in this case on May 30, 2012. Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. See Chretien v. Patients Mutual Assistance Collective Corp. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: COBASAC LLC VS. REBECCA HILL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Quash Service Of Unlawfule Detainer Summons; On First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 31, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT REBECCA HILL's Motion To Quash Service Of Unlawfule Detainer Summons DENIED, 5 days to respond. Complaint sufficiently alleges cause of action for unlawful detainer and is compliant with CCP 1166. = (501/REQ)
